title: Rhassoul clay melt-and-pour soap
subtitle: A gentle detoxifying bar with rhassoul clay and lavender in a pre-made soap base
excerpt: Melt-and-pour soap base removes the lye handling step entirely. Rhassoul clay is added at 3% of base weight -- enough to give the bar mild drawing and cleansing properties without over-drying. Lavender essential oil is stable at pour temperatures and scents the bar reliably.
sourceNotes: Melt-and-pour soap base formulation and pour temperature: cosmetic craft reference. Rhassoul clay usage rate in M&P soap (2-3% of base weight): candle and soap formulation practice. Lavender essential oil stability in glycerin soap base: standard cosmetic craft reference.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep clay at 3% and essential oil at 2% of base weight.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow bars to set fully (at least 2 hours) before unmoulding. Cure is not required for melt-and-pour soap -- bars are ready to use once firm.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keep for one year in a cool, dry place. Melt-and-pour bars absorb moisture from the air -- wrap individually in clingfilm or store in a tin to prevent sweating.

glossaryTerms.definition: A volcanic clay from the Atlas Mountains of Morocco. Mildly drawing and mineral-rich, with a finer texture than bentonite. Suitable for sensitive skin in soap and face masks at low concentrations.

content.text: Melt-and-pour soap is the most accessible soap-making route: the base is already saponified, so no lye calculation or safety precautions are needed. Adding
gives the bar a light clay colour and a mild drawing action that suits oily and congested skin. Stir the clay in just before pouring -- adding it too early can cause it to settle.

Cut the soap base into rough cubes. Melt in a heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water, stirring occasionally. Alternatively, melt in the microwave in 30-second bursts, stirring between each. Do not overheat -- you want the base just melted, not bubbling. Remove from the heat and cool to about 60°C.

Weigh the rhassoul clay into a small bowl and mix with 1 teaspoon of the melted base to form a smooth paste. Stir the paste into the main batch -- this prevents lumps. Add the lavender essential oil if using and stir gently. Avoid vigorous stirring, which introduces bubbles.

Pour steadily into the silicone loaf mould. If bubbles appear on the surface, mist lightly with isopropyl alcohol -- this pops them and gives a smoother top. Leave undisturbed at room temperature for at least two hours. Unmould and slice with the wire cutter into 6 to 8 bars.

Melt-and-pour bars are hygroscopic and will develop a wet, sweating surface if left exposed to air. Wrap each bar individually in clingfilm, or pack into a tin or box. The bars are ready to use immediately -- no cure is needed.
